content: I'm so stressed about my mom's upcoming surgery. She's having a hip replacement next week and I'm worried sick about her.
I'm really sorry you're going through this. Hip replacements can be tough, but they often provide great relief. What's making you most anxious about it?
She's 78 and lives alone in San Antonio. I'm her only child and I work crazy hours at the ER, so I can't be there with her as much as I'd like.
That sounds incredibly difficult. Being a nurse yourself, you know exactly what the recovery entails. Have you arranged for any help for her during the recovery period?
I hired a part-time caregiver through an agency, but I still feel guilty. My YouTube channel is taking off too, and I'm trying to balance everything.
It's amazing that you're managing so much - your career, caring for your mom, and building your YouTube channel. That's a lot on your plate. What kind of content are you creating?
It's mostly philosophy discussions and book reviews. I started it as a creative outlet, but now I have over 10,000 subscribers! Still, I feel like I should be doing more for Mom.
10,000 subscribers is fantastic! You should be proud of that achievement. For your mom, maybe you could set up video calls during your breaks at the hospital? Even short check-ins can mean a lot.
That's actually a good idea. She's not very tech-savvy though - I had to teach her Zoom last month and it was painful. But I could call her the old-fashioned way.
Regular phone calls would be perfect. And maybe you could record some piano pieces for her? I remember you mentioning that's one of your hobbies.
She does love when I play. I could do that. I just hate that I can't be there in person more. The hospital keeps scheduling me for extra shifts because we're short-staffed.
That's so tough - wanting to be there for your mom but also needing to work. Have you talked to your supervisor about your situation? They might be able to adjust your schedule.
I haven't yet. I guess I should. I'm just worried they'll think I'm not committed. But family comes first, right? I'll talk to them tomorrow.
